process_matches.reason: Both describe near-rapid solidification by centrifugal casting with identical parameters (600 r/min, Ar, ~5e3 K/s cooling, same strip dimensions).
Both describe annealing at 550°C for 5 h in vacuum electric resistance furnace.

truth.papers.A15.title: Microstructure and mechanical properties of a Fe–28Mn–9Al-1.2C-(0, 3, 6, 9)Cr austenitic low-density steel
truth.papers.A15.paper_scope: Investigate the effects of Cr addition (0-9 wt%) on microstructure, carbide formation, and mechanical properties of Fe-28Mn-9Al-1.2C strips fabricated under near-rapid solidification, and the effect of annealing at 550 °C for 5 h.

prediction.papers.A15.title: Microstructure and mechanical properties of a Fe–28Mn–9Al-1.2C-(0, 3, 6, 9)Cr austenitic low-density steel
prediction.papers.A15.paper_scope: Investigate effects of Cr addition (0–9 wt%) on microstructure, carbide formation and mechanical properties of Fe–28Mn–9Al–1.2C low-density steel strips fabricated under near-rapid solidification condition, including as-cast and annealed (550°C for 5 h) states.

truth.processes.proc_as_cast.description: Near-rapid solidification by centrifugal casting: alloy melt poured into a copper mold rotating at 600 r/min in Ar atmosphere; estimated maximum cooling rate ~5×10^3 K/s; resulting strip dimensions (60-80)×60×2.5 mm.
truth.processes.proc_annealed_1h.description: Annealing at 550 °C for 1 h in a vacuum electric resistance furnace.
truth.processes.proc_annealed_5h.description: Annealing at 550 °C for 5 h in a vacuum electric resistance furnace.
truth.processes.proc_annealed_9h.description: Annealing at 550 °C for 9 h in a vacuum electric resistance furnace.

prediction.processes.proc_as_cast.description: Near-rapid solidification by centrifugal casting into copper mold at 600 r/min in Ar atmosphere; calculated maximum cooling rate about 5×10³ K/s; strip size (60–80) mm × 60 mm × 2.5 mm.
prediction.processes.proc_as_cast.processes_notes: Shared as-cast process for all studied strips. Alloy ingot reheated to melt by induction coil before pouring.
prediction.processes.proc_annealed_5h.description: Annealing at 550°C for 5 h in a vacuum electric resistance furnace.
prediction.processes.proc_annealed_5h.processes_not: Shared annealing process used to compare properties of all strips after heat treatment.

truth.structures.sample_0cr_as_cast.overall_st: Austenite matrix with 10.2 vol% ferrite, nano-scale κ-carbides in austenite, low dislocation density in austenite
truth.structures.sample_0cr_as_cast.microstruc: ferrite volume fraction 10.2 vol%; austenite lattice constant 3.70 Å (XRD)
nano-scale, visible at phase boundary in DF images
rodlike, located at center of austenite grains
truth.structures.sample_0cr_annealed_5h.overal: Austenite matrix with ferrite, enhanced κ-carbide precipitation, reduced dislocation density
truth.structures.sample_0cr_annealed_5h.micros: ferrite fraction similar to as-cast; κ-carbide peak (111) at 41.4° appears in XRD
increased volume fraction, superlattice intensity increased
truth.structures.sample_3cr_as_cast.overall_st: Austenite matrix with 11.4 vol% ferrite, very weak κ-carbides, increased dislocation density in austenite
truth.structures.sample_3cr_as_cast.microstruc: ferrite volume fraction 11.4 vol%; no DO3 in ferrite; austenite lattice constant 3.56 Å
very weak superlattice, size too small to identify
truth.structures.sample_3cr_annealed_5h.overal: Austenite matrix with ferrite, very weak κ-carbides, reduced dislocation density compared with as-cast, DO3 in ferrite
truth.structures.sample_3cr_annealed_5h.micros: do3 superlattice appears in ferrite; κ-carbide superlattice still very weak
reduced compared to as-cast, still higher than annealed 0Cr
truth.structures.sample_3cr_annealed_1h.overal: Austenite matrix with ferrite, κ-carbides mainly precipitated in first hour, dislocation density still high but decreasing
truth.structures.sample_3cr_annealed_1h.micros: Based on Fig. 11(b): dislocation density high
truth.structures.sample_3cr_annealed_9h.overal: Austenite matrix with ferrite, lowest dislocation density after longer annealing, κ-carbides present
truth.structures.sample_3cr_annealed_9h.micros: Based on Fig. 11(d): dislocation density low
truth.structures.sample_6cr_as_cast.overall_st: Austenite matrix with 14.8 vol% ferrite, weak κ-carbides, high dislocation density, DO3 in ferrite
truth.structures.sample_6cr_as_cast.microstruc: ferrite volume fraction 14.8 vol%; austenite lattice constant 3.56 Å; DO3 nano-precipitates in ferrite
very weak superlattice, size too small, less than 0Cr
truth.structures.sample_6cr_annealed_5h.overal: Austenite matrix with ferrite, weak κ-carbides, reduced dislocation density, DO3 in ferrite
truth.structures.sample_6cr_annealed_5h.micros: dislocation density reduced; κ still weak; ferrite volume fraction ~14.8%
reduced compared to as-cast 6Cr, but still higher than annealed 0Cr
truth.structures.sample_9cr_as_cast.overall_st: Austenite matrix with 19.6 vol% ferrite, no κ-carbides, Cr23C6 at grain boundaries, high dislocation density, DO3 in ferrite
truth.structures.sample_9cr_as_cast.microstruc: ferrite volume fraction 19.6 vol%; austenite lattice constant 3.55 Å; Cr23C6 width ~100 nm
granular or banded at austenite grain boundaries
truth.structures.sample_9cr_annealed_5h.overal: Austenite matrix with ferrite, no κ-carbides, larger Cr23C6 at grain boundaries, reduced dislocation density, DO3 in ferrite
truth.structures.sample_9cr_annealed_5h.micros: Cr23C6 width ~200 nm; no κ; ferrite volume fraction ~19.6%
reduced compared to as-cast, still higher than annealed 0Cr

prediction.structures.sample_0cr_as_cast.overall_st: Austenite matrix with uniformly distributed δ-ferrite and nano-scale κ-carbide precipitates; ferrite nucleated in austenite grain centers indicating FA solidification mode.
prediction.structures.sample_0cr_as_cast.microstruc: at austenite/ferrite phase boundary; superlattice visible in SADP
prediction.structures.sample_0cr_annealed_5h.overal: Austenite and ferrite retained after annealing; increased κ-carbide precipitation in austenite; DO3 superlattice appears in ferrite after annealing.
prediction.structures.sample_0cr_annealed_5h.micros: matrix with clear grain boundaries and dendritic structure
in austenite matrix; superlattice intensity increased in SADP
prediction.structures.sample_3cr_as_cast.overall_st: Austenite matrix with δ-ferrite; weak κ-carbide superlattice; increased dislocation density compared to 0Cr.
prediction.structures.sample_3cr_as_cast.microstruc: very weak superlattice in austenite SADP; suppressed precipitation
prediction.structures.sample_3cr_annealed_5h.overal: Austenite and ferrite; κ-carbide superlattice still very weak; dislocation density reduced after annealing; DO3 phase observed in ferrite.
prediction.structures.sample_3cr_annealed_5h.micros: matrix; grain boundaries not clearly observed
suppressed by Cr addition even after annealing
prediction.structures.sample_6cr_as_cast.overall_st: Austenite matrix with δ-ferrite; very weak κ-carbide superlattice; DO3 phase in ferrite; high dislocation density.
prediction.structures.sample_6cr_as_cast.microstruc: very weak superlattice in austenite SADP
prediction.structures.sample_6cr_annealed_5h.overal: Austenite and ferrite; weak κ-carbide superlattice; reduced dislocation density after annealing; DO3 present in ferrite.
prediction.structures.sample_6cr_annealed_5h.micros: reduced compared to as-cast 6Cr; still higher than annealed 0Cr
very weak superlattice; content lower than 0Cr
prediction.structures.sample_9cr_as_cast.overall_st: Austenite matrix with increased δ-ferrite fraction; no κ-carbide superlattice; Cr23C6 carbides at austenite grain boundaries; high dislocation density slightly lower than 6Cr.
prediction.structures.sample_9cr_as_cast.microstruc: at austenite grain boundaries; granular or banded shapes; cubic structure with lattice constant 1.054 nm
prediction.structures.sample_9cr_annealed_5h.overal: Austenite and ferrite; no κ-carbide superlattice; Cr23C6 carbides remain at grain boundaries; DO3 in ferrite; dislocation density reduced after annealing.

prediction.interfaces.sample_0cr_as_cast.defect_int: κ-carbides obstruct dislocation glide and generate slip bands; glide plane softening effect yields lower strain hardening rate
prediction.interfaces.sample_0cr_as_cast.interface_: Slip bands observed during deformation; average slip band spacing ~90 nm after tensile fracture in as-cast 0Cr.
prediction.interfaces.sample_6cr_as_cast.defect_int: dislocation blocking by reduced κ-carbides and high dislocation density
Reduced nano-scale κ-carbides result in high strain hardening rate; increased dislocation density and phase-interface areas block dislocation propagation and reduce plasticity.
prediction.interfaces.sample_6cr_as_cast.interface_: Slip bands observed; average spacing ~80 nm after tensile fracture in as-cast 6Cr; multi-directional shearing occurs due to smaller and less κ-carbides.
prediction.interfaces.sample_9cr_as_cast.defect_int: Cr23C6 carbides at austenite grain boundaries may lead to inter-granular cracking and are a reason for the lowest ductility.
prediction.interfaces.sample_0cr_annealed_5h.defect: Coarse κ-carbides (average 14 nm) act as barriers because dislocations can only pass through those smaller than 10 nm, reducing slip band formation and total elongation.
prediction.interfaces.sample_0cr_annealed_5h.interf: Average slip band spacing increases to ~190 nm after tensile fracture; slip band width 20.5 nm.
prediction.interfaces.sample_6cr_annealed_5h.defect: Less and smaller κ-carbides can be easily passed through by dislocations; thermal stress and dislocations partially disappear after annealing, allowing more slip bands.
prediction.interfaces.sample_6cr_annealed_5h.interf: Average slip band spacing decreases to ~60 nm after tensile fracture; slip band width 7.9 nm; dynamic slip band refinement (DSBR) observed.

truth.properties.sample_3cr_annealed_5h.mechan: reduced by ~500 MPa compared with as-cast MPa

truth.performance.sample_3cr_as_cast.comparati: Yield strength decreased by 107 MPa, UTS decreased by 28 MPa, total elongation decreased by 7.6%
truth.performance.sample_6cr_as_cast.comparati: Yield strength decreased by 72 MPa, UTS increased by 29 MPa, total elongation decreased by 9.2%
truth.performance.sample_9cr_as_cast.comparati: Yield strength increased by 16 MPa, UTS increased by 55 MPa, total elongation decreased by 20.5%
truth.performance.sample_3cr_annealed_5h.compa: Yield strength decreased by 114 MPa, UTS decreased by 19 MPa, total elongation increased by 7.3%
truth.performance.sample_6cr_annealed_5h.compa: Yield strength decreased by 131 MPa, UTS increased by 31 MPa, total elongation increased by 14.2%
truth.performance.sample_9cr_annealed_5h.compa: Yield strength decreased by 77 MPa, UTS increased by 60 MPa, total elongation decreased by 7.3%

After 1 h: high dislocation density. After 5 h: reduced. After 9 h: further reduced.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_0cr_as_c: SJ-600G Electronic Water Displacement Density Analyzer
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_0cr_anne: Tensile testing (MTS Criterion Model 44, 10 kN, strain rate 1×10⁻³ s⁻¹)
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_3cr_as_c: XRD / OM / TEM / Tensile test / Density measurement
Austenite lattice constant 3.56 Å; ferrite fraction 11.4 vol%; increased dislocation density; weak κ-carbide superlattice; YS 617 ± 4 MPa, UTS 896 ± 28 MPa, TE 33.2 ± 7 %; density 6.45 g/cm³.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_3cr_anne: Microstructure and mechanical characterization after annealing
κ-carbide superlattice still very weak; dislocation density reduced; DO3 in ferrite; YS 768 ± 9 MPa, UTS 915 ± 23 MPa, TE 37.4 ± 5 %.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_6cr_as_c: XRD / OM / TEM / Tensile test / Density measurement
Austenite lattice constant 3.56 Å; ferrite fraction 14.8 vol%; DO3 phase in ferrite; high dislocation density; YS 652 ± 5 MPa, UTS 953 ± 9 MPa, TE 31.6 ± 1 %; density 6.46 g/cm³.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_6cr_anne: Microstructure and mechanical characterization after annealing
Dislocation density reduced; DO3 in ferrite; small and less κ-carbides; YS 751 ± 2 MPa, UTS 965 ± 2 MPa, TE 44.3 ± 3 %.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_9cr_as_c: XRD / OM / TEM / Tensile test / Density measurement

Austenite lattice constant 3.55 Å; ferrite fraction 19.6 vol%; DO3 in ferrite; Cr23C6 at austenite grain boundaries (width ~100 nm); no κ-carbide superlattice; YS 740 ± 8 MPa, UTS 979 ± 22 MPa, TE 20.3 ± 5 %; density 6.43 g/cm³.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_9cr_anne: Microstructure and mechanical characterization after annealing
Cr23C6 carbides at austenite grain boundaries (width ~200 nm); no κ-carbide; DO3 in ferrite; YS 805 ± 11 MPa, UTS 994 ± 21 MPa, TE 22.8 ± 4 %.

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The solidification mode follows FA: L → L+δ → L+δ+γ → δ+γ. Ferrite nucleates first at grain centers.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Dislocation density in Cr-containing as-cast strips is higher due to reduced κ-carbides which hinder dislocation formation under thermal stress, combined with increased solid solubility of C from Cr addition. In 9Cr, Cr23C6 precipitation reduces dislocation formation.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: The glide plane softening effect of nano κ-carbides leads to lower strain hardening rate in 0Cr. The disappearance of κ and increased dislocations in Cr-containing strips increase strain hardening rate.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: After annealing, the unusual plasticity behavior (0Cr TE decreases, Cr-containing TE increases except 9Cr) is explained by the balance between κ precipitation (increases YS, reduces plasticity) and dislocation recovery (increases plasticity). In 6Cr, limited κ precipitation and dislocation recovery result in the highest annealed elongation.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: The average widths of slip bands: as-cast 0Cr 13.4 nm, as-cast 6Cr 12.5 nm, annealed 0Cr 20.5 nm, annealed 6Cr 7.9 nm. Finer slip bands with smaller spacing after tensile fracture associated with high ductility (dynamic slip band refinement), but initial dislocation density also strongly influences elongation.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: For 3Cr steel, annealing at 550 °C for 1 h mainly precipitates κ and increases YS; longer time (9 h) reduces dislocation density and improves plasticity but risks coarsening κ. Optimal annealing time needs further optimization to balance coarsening and recovery.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Cr addition improves corrosion resistance (evidenced by clearer grain boundary observation in 0Cr after annealing but not in Cr-containing strips).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: The average density measured for 0Cr, 3Cr, 6Cr, 9Cr strips is 6.49, 6.45, 6.46, 6.43 g/cm3 respectively.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Fig. 11 examines 3Cr strips annealed at 550°C for 1 h, 5 h and 9 h: plasticity increases gradually with annealing time, while yield strength does not change significantly after 1 h; TEM shows dislocation density gradually decreases with increasing annealing time; κ-carbides mainly precipitate in the first hour while recovery needs longer time.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Slip band statistics after tensile fracture: as-cast 0Cr average spacing ~90 nm and width 13.4 nm; as-cast 6Cr average spacing ~80 nm and width 12.5 nm; annealed 0Cr average spacing ~190 nm and width 20.5 nm; annealed 6Cr average spacing ~60 nm and width 7.9 nm. Finer slip bands and smaller spacing correspond to higher ductility (dynamic slip band refinement, DSBR), but high initial dislocation density in as-cast 6Cr interferes with this relationship.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Austenite lattice constants measured by XRD/JADE: 0Cr 3.70 Å, 3Cr 3.56 Å, 6Cr 3.56 Å, 9Cr 3.55 Å.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Fig. 3(a) engineering stress-strain curves and Fig. 3(b) true stress-strain and strain hardening rate curves for as-cast strips are referenced but exact curve coordinates beyond tabulated values are not fully extractable from text.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Fig. 8(a) and 8(b) provide engineering and true stress-strain curves for annealed strips; strain hardening rate of all annealed strips except 9Cr clearly decreases compared with as-cast, with 3Cr showing the largest reduction about 500 MPa.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Cr addition improves corrosion resistance of steel strips, as evidenced by grain boundaries being less visible in Cr-containing strips under OM after annealing, consistent with literature reports by Tsay and Tuan.
